evals.prompt: check my requirements.txt and explain anything that looks wrong
evals.expected_output: An offline JSON-backed diagnosis of the selected requirements.txt, followed by plain-language findings and suggested fixes.
expectations: The skill runs scripts/dep_doctor.py on the named manifest with --json and without --online
Each finding includes severity, kind, package, line, why, and fix
The answer distinguishes standard-library pins, obsolete backports, unpinned dependencies, duplicates, and exact-pin conflicts
No manifest edit is made without the user's approval

evals.prompt: why won't my deps install? please autopsy pyproject.toml
evals.expected_output: A local diagnosis of pyproject.toml that leads with high-severity manifest problems and states the limits of the check.
expectations: The selected pyproject.toml is inspected instead of sweeping the repository
High-severity findings are explained before medium or low findings
The answer does not claim to be a complete dependency resolver or CVE audit
The skill offers a focused edit only after presenting the diagnosis

evals.prompt: is this exact package version yanked on PyPI?
evals.expected_output: A permission check before any network access, then an opt-in PyPI yank lookup for the exact Python pin.
expectations: The skill asks for permission before passing --online
Only package names and exact pinned versions are sent to pypi.org
A release is called yanked only when all of its PyPI files are marked yanked
The answer does not equate a yank with a CVE
